tool_args.label: New Fare Payment System (NFPS) / OMNY
tool_args.raw_text: The NYCT New Fare Payment System provides for the replacement of the MetroCard fare collection system...with a state-of-the-art contactless fare collection system.

payload.description: Multi-year design-build program replacing MetroCard with OMNY contactless fare collection system, including CVMs, OMNY validators, and closed-loop card distribution

tool_args.label: Railroad New Fare Payment System (RRNFPS)
tool_args.raw_text: Railroad New Fare Payment System (RRNFPS)

payload.description: Replacement of legacy fare collection at LIRR and MNR with new TVMs, TOMs, back-office software, mobile ticketing, and OSVD

tool_args.label: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2
tool_args.raw_text: two contracts for Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 advancing to the Board this month

payload.description: Program management contract with financial incentives and penalties; design contract converted to design-bid-build to minimize risk and save tens of millions of dollars

tool_args.label: CBTC Queens Boulevard West Line - Phase 1
tool_args.raw_text: Contract S-48004-1 provides Communication Based Train Control ("CBTC") equipment for the CBTC signaling project on the Queens Boulevard West Line.

payload.project_name: CBTC Queens Boulevard West Line - Phase 1
payload.description: CBTC signaling project on the Queens Boulevard West Line with Siemens, including database validation tool, cloud-based ATS expansion tool, and carborne controller software modifications

tool_args.label: Fulton CBTC Procurement / Liberty Line Signal Replacement
tool_args.raw_text: the project to replace the 79-year-old signaling equipment on the Liberty Line, is moving forward, with the RFQ already released and the RFP expected within weeks. This project is part of the Fulton CBTC procurement

payload.project_name: Fulton CBTC Procurement / Liberty Line Signal Replacement
payload.description: Replacement of 79-year-old signaling equipment on the Liberty Line, part of Fulton CBTC procurement, bundling projects across capital plans for efficiency
